description_long: This object is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a rough, angular profile and a predominantly gray-green coloration. Its surface is uneven, featuring jagged edges and fractured planes, with a coarse, granular texture typical of natural rock. A small, white rectangular label with printed text is affixed to one of its flatter faces, suggesting it may be cataloged or used for identification purposes. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or significant weathering, indicating it has not been exposed to the elements for an extended period. Subtle variations in color and texture are visible across its surface, hinting at mineral inclusions or slight compositional differences. This type of stone could commonly be found as a loose obstacle or barrier in urban parks, roadside verges, or landscaped public spaces.
description: A rough, angular gray-green stone with a coarse texture and a small white label affixed to one face. Its jagged edges and solid appearance make it suitable as an urban obstacle or barrier.
description_view_0: The stone is seen from an oblique angle, highlighting its broadest face with the white label clearly visible and showing the overall irregular, blocky shape.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the flatter upper surface with the label, revealing more of the stone's granular texture and less of its thickness.
description_view_2: A side profile shows the stone's thin, wedge-like silhouette, making its angular taper and sharp edges more apparent.
description_view_3: The end view displays the stone's thickest cross-section, with a nearly vertical face and visible fractures along the edge.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or loose obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and granular texture with no regular pattern; the surface is naturally irregular with minor mineral speckling.
attribute_weathering: Freshly broken with sharp edges and minimal surface smoothing, indicating little exposure to weathering or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically contrasts with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, but may blend with other natural stones or gravel in landscaped areas.
